element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns single-screen two-player system where staying ahead keeps opponent off-screen
Leaving defined route sends player back to track; reinforces path adherence
Obstacles introduce collision avoidance beyond racing opponents

element_introduction_order.element: Vehicle-specific abilities (tanks with shooting, helicopters, boats)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Character selection adds tactical depth; some vehicles have inherent advantages

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Every three races in Challenge Mode
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Winning time trials grants extra lives; shifts from head-to-head to time-attack gameplay
Players must place 1st or 2nd to advance; three failures end game

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully finishing 1st or 2nd in a race
Winning time trial to gain extra life
Clearing a race without off-track penalties
Head-to-head victory when leading player accumulates 8 lights
typical_tension_triggers: Racing against computer AI opponents in close proximity
Approaching track boundaries to avoid reset penalty
Managing limited tries (three chances before game over)
Opponent gaining screen advantage in head-to-head mode
Navigating dense obstacle fields on household environment tracks
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Short track design creates rapid challenge cycles; three-strike structure maintains persistent tension while time trials provide breathing room every three races

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Head-to-head distance mechanic: trailing player off-screen signals point loss; spatial separation equals victory condition

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to single-screen racing system and track boundaries; basic obstacle avoidance on themed environments
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased AI opponent competitiveness; more complex obstacle layouts; character selection strategy emerges
notable_spikes: First transition from beginner-friendly tracks to obstacle-dense environments
Time trial races introducing time-attack pressure separate from opponent racing
Handicap system in two-player mode adjusts for character selection balance

notable_level_patterns.description: Tracks set on breakfast tables, pool tables, desks, and treehouses; environments contain thematic obstacles (cereal boxes, pencil sharpeners) rather than generic barriers
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Breakfast table with cereal box obstacles

notable_level_patterns.description: Both players confined to one screen; no split-screen; victory determined by screen position rather than lap time—trailing player off-screen equals point loss
Courses have mandatory racing lines; deviation beyond tolerance triggers immediate reset to track, preventing creative routing
Every third race switches to time-attack format; winning grants extra life; creates meta-rhythm of three races then relief opportunity

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Micro Machines NES)
